name: Discover Skill Capabilities
query: What can you help me do with Conductor?
expected_behavior: Step 1: Activate the conductor skill from natural-language intent (no slash command typed)
Step 2: Summarize the major capability groups from SKILL.md: workflow CRUD, execution, monitoring, management, signaling, scheduling, worker scaffolding, visualization, review/optimization
Step 3: Mention that schedules are part of OSS (not Orkes-only) and that secrets/webhooks are Orkes-only
Step 4: Offer concrete sample requests the user can make (e.g. 'create a workflow that...', 'review my workflow', 'schedule X daily')
Step 5: Optionally mention the slash-command shortcuts that exist on Claude Code (/conductor-setup, /conductor-optimize, /conductor-scaffold-worker) but emphasize plain English works too
Step 6: Ask what the user wants to do next
success_criteria: Agent does not require a slash command to discover capabilities — natural language triggers the skill
Major capability areas (create, run, monitor, manage, signal, schedule, optimize, visualize, workers) are all mentioned at least once
Schedules are correctly described as OSS, not Orkes-only
Sample queries are concrete and actionable, not vague
Agent ends by asking what the user wants to do (not by dumping the entire skill content)
